title: AVS co_change Precision Hardening via FTA-driven Three-MCS Fix (ADR-018)

features.description: Same-directory guard in _check_co_change suppresses sister-file co-evolution in package directories via PurePosixPath.parent comparison. Root-level files (parent == '.') are NOT suppressed to preserve flat-repo detection. SPOF: RPN 144 -> 24.

known_files built from filtered_prs instead of parse_results — consistent with the import graph that also uses filtered_prs. Previously test files were in known but not in graph, making has_edge always False for test-source pairs. SPOF: RPN 60 -> 10.

Commit-size discount weight = 1.0 / max(1, len(files) - 1) in build_co_change_pairs. Surgical 2-file commits count as 1.0; 15-file sweep commits count as ~0.07. Reduces inflated confidence from release/FMEA-sweep commits. Hard >20 cap retained. RPN 120 -> 30.

mutation_benchmark.note: AVS mutation benchmark tests avs_001 (upward import) and avs_002 (transitive layer violation) — both avs_upward_import mutations unaffected. co_change recall not measurable via synthetic commit injection.

precision_improvement.basis: precision_strict=0.3 from drift_self sample (n=20, 10/10 Disputed). After fix: avs_co_change=0 in self-analysis; all 3 MCS barriers passed; 4 regression tests green.
precision_improvement.evidence_quality: FTA-based causal tracing + operational regression tests + self-analysis zero-finding confirmation

evidence_summary: AVS co_change FPs fully eliminated in self-analysis (20->0 findings, score 0.522->0.501). AVS mutation recall preserved at 100% (2/2). All 97 ground truth fixtures passing. 5 regression tests added covering all 3 MCS plus FN guard. MCS-4 (models layer) remains open/latent.
